result.summary: WorkOS exposes a REST API at https://api.workos.com, a hosted Management MCP server at https://mcp.workos.com/mcp, and a WorkOS CLI distributed via npm; the REST API uses bearer secret API keys, while the MCP server and CLI use WorkOS OAuth/dashboard sign-in.
result.description: WorkOS provides enterprise authentication and identity infrastructure for SaaS applications, including user management, enterprise SSO, directory sync, authorization, audit logs, and related admin tooling. It also offers AI-agent access to account management through a hosted MCP server.

workos_api_key.label: WorkOS API key (secret key)
workos_api_key.setup: Sign in to the [WorkOS Dashboard](https://dashboard.workos.com/), open [API Keys](https://dashboard.workos.com/api-keys), and create or copy a secret API key. The API reference shows these keys used as `Authorization: Bearer <secret_key>`.

workos_dashboard_oauth.label: WorkOS dashboard account OAuth grant
workos_dashboard_oauth.setup: Connect an MCP client to the hosted WorkOS MCP server at [WorkOS MCP docs](https://workos.com/docs/mcp.md). On first connection, the client opens a WorkOS consent screen where you sign in with your WorkOS dashboard account and approve access; the client then stores the OAuth grant/token it received from WorkOS Connect.
